text: Add a new contact named 'Dr. Emily Watson' with phone number 555-0142 and email emily.watson@hospital.org to the Contacts app.
screenshots:
Screen 1: Contacts app main view in portrait orientation. Top bar shows 'Contacts' title with search icon and three-dot menu. A floating action button (blue circle with '+' icon) is in the bottom-right corner. The contact list shows 12 existing contacts sorted alphabetically with circular avatar icons and names.

Screen 2: New contact form is displayed. Fields visible from top to bottom: profile photo placeholder (camera icon in a circle), First name, Last name, Company, Phone (with phone icon), Email (with envelope icon). The keyboard is open at the bottom of the screen. 'First name' field is focused with a blinking cursor.

Screen 3: Contact form partially filled. First name: 'Emily', Last name: 'Watson', Name prefix field added showing 'Dr.' Phone field shows '555-0142' with type label 'Mobile'. Email field shows 'emily.watson@hospital.org' with type label 'Work'. The 'Save' button (checkmark icon) is visible in the top-right corner.

Screen 4: Contact detail view for 'Dr. Emily Watson'. The screen shows the contact card with name at top, phone number 555-0142 with call/message action buttons, and email emily.watson@hospital.org with email action button. A green banner at top briefly shows 'Contact saved'.
trajectory:
Step 1: [TAP] Floating action button ('+' icon) in bottom-right corner
Step 2: [TAP] Name prefix field, [TYPE] 'Dr.'
Step 3: [TAP] First name field, [TYPE] 'Emily'
Step 4: [TAP] Last name field, [TYPE] 'Watson'
Step 5: [SCROLL] Down to reveal phone field
Step 6: [TAP] Phone field, [TYPE] '555-0142'
Step 7: [TAP] Email field, [TYPE] 'emily.watson@hospital.org'
Step 8: [TAP] Email type label, select 'Work' from dropdown
Step 9: [TAP] Save button (checkmark icon) in top-right
Step 10: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Contact detail screen showing 'Dr. Emily Watson'. Phone: 555-0142 (Mobile). Email: emily.watson@hospital.org (Work). Contact successfully saved and visible in the contacts list. Save confirmation banner displayed.

text: Create a calendar event for 'Team Standup' recurring every weekday at 9:00 AM for 15 minutes in the Calendar app.
screenshots:
Screen 1: Google Calendar app in month view. The current month is displayed with colored dots indicating existing events on various dates. The floating action button ('+' icon) is in the bottom-right corner. The navigation drawer icon (hamburger menu) is in the top-left. The current date is highlighted with a blue circle.

Screen 2: New event creation screen. Title field at top shows 'Team Standup' typed in. Below: date picker showing today's date, start time set to '9:00 AM', end time set to '9:15 AM'. 'Add people' field, 'Add location' field, 'Add notification' option, and 'More options' link are visible. The 'Does not repeat' dropdown is shown below the date.

Screen 3: Recurrence picker dialog is open, overlaying the event creation screen. Options listed: 'Does not repeat', 'Every day', 'Every week', 'Every month', 'Every year', 'Custom...'. The agent has tapped 'Custom...'. A custom recurrence dialog shows: Repeat every 1 week, with checkboxes for days — Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri are all checked (highlighted in blue). Sat and Sun are unchecked.

Screen 4: Back on the event creation screen. The recurrence field now shows 'Every weekday (Mon-Fri)'. All other fields are populated. The 'Save' button is visible in the top-right corner. The event summary reads: Team Standup, 9:00 AM - 9:15 AM, Every weekday.
trajectory:
Step 1: [TAP] Floating action button ('+') in bottom-right
Step 2: [TAP] Title field, [TYPE] 'Team Standup'
Step 3: [TAP] Start time field
Step 4: [TAP] Hour selector to '9', minute to '00', AM selected
Step 5: [TAP] End time field
Step 6: [TAP] Hour selector to '9', minute to '15', AM selected
Step 7: [TAP] 'Does not repeat' dropdown
Step 8: [TAP] 'Custom...' option
Step 9: [TAP] Checkboxes for Mon, Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri
Step 10: [TAP] 'Done' to confirm recurrence
Step 11: [TAP] 'Save' button in top-right
Step 12: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Google Calendar returns to month view. The newly created 'Team Standup' event appears on all weekdays. Event details: 9:00 AM - 9:15 AM, recurring every weekday (Mon-Fri). No end date set for the recurrence.

text: In the Messages app, send a text message to the contact 'Alex Kim' saying 'Running 10 minutes late to lunch, sorry!'
screenshots:
Screen 1: Messages app main screen showing a list of recent conversations. Each conversation shows a contact avatar, name, message preview, and timestamp. Visible conversations include: Mom (2 min ago), Alex Kim (1 hour ago), Work Group (3 hours ago), Sarah J (yesterday). The search bar is at the top and a compose button (pencil icon) is in the bottom-right.

Screen 2: Conversation thread with Alex Kim is open. Previous messages visible: Alex sent 'Are we still on for lunch at noon?' (11:30 AM). The user's previous reply 'Yes! See you at the usual place' (11:35 AM) is shown in a blue bubble on the right. The message input field is at the bottom with a text cursor, camera icon, and send button (arrow icon).

Screen 3: The message input field now contains the typed text 'Running 10 minutes late to lunch, sorry!' The on-screen keyboard is visible at the bottom half of the screen. The send button (blue arrow icon) is highlighted and ready to tap.

Screen 4: The message 'Running 10 minutes late to lunch, sorry!' appears as the newest message in a blue bubble on the right side. Timestamp shows 'Just now'. A small delivery status indicator (single checkmark) appears below the message bubble. The keyboard has been dismissed.
trajectory:
Step 1: [TAP] Conversation with 'Alex Kim' in the messages list
Step 2: [TAP] Message input field at bottom of screen
Step 3: [TYPE] 'Running 10 minutes late to lunch, sorry!'
Step 4: [TAP] Send button (blue arrow icon)
Step 5: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Conversation with Alex Kim shows the new message 'Running 10 minutes late to lunch, sorry!' delivered as the latest message. Delivery status shows single checkmark (sent). The conversation thread displays the full message history.

text: Open the Settings app and enable Do Not Disturb mode with an exception for calls from starred contacts only.
screenshots:
Screen 1: Settings app main screen. A scrollable list of setting categories is shown with icons: Network & internet, Connected devices, Apps, Notifications, Battery, Storage, Sound & vibration, Display, Wallpaper & style, Accessibility, Security & privacy, Location, Safety & emergency, System. The search bar is at the top.

Screen 2: 'Sound & vibration' settings screen. Options shown: Media volume slider, Call volume slider, Ring & notification volume slider, Alarm volume slider. Below the sliders: 'Do Not Disturb' toggle (currently OFF), 'Phone ringtone', 'Vibration & haptics'. The agent taps on 'Do Not Disturb'.

Screen 3: Do Not Disturb settings screen. Toggle at top is now ON (blue). Sections below: 'People' (Conversations, Calls, Messages), 'Apps', 'Alarms & other interruptions', 'Schedules', 'Duration'. Under 'Calls', it shows 'From anyone'. The agent taps on 'Calls' to modify the exception.

Screen 4: Calls exception screen. Radio button options: 'From anyone', 'From contacts only', 'From starred contacts only', 'None'. The agent has selected 'From starred contacts only' (radio button filled in blue). A back arrow is in the top-left corner to return to DND settings.
trajectory:
Step 1: [TAP] 'Sound & vibration' in Settings main menu
Step 2: [SCROLL] Down to find 'Do Not Disturb'
Step 3: [TAP] 'Do Not Disturb' option
Step 4: [TAP] Do Not Disturb toggle to enable (turn ON)
Step 5: [TAP] 'Calls' under People section
Step 6: [TAP] 'From starred contacts only' radio button
Step 7: [TAP] Back arrow to return to DND settings
Step 8: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Do Not Disturb is enabled (toggle ON). Call exception set to 'From starred contacts only'. Status bar at top of screen shows the DND icon (circle with horizontal line). All other notifications are silenced except calls from starred contacts.

text: Use Google Maps to search for the nearest pharmacy, get directions, and save the location to the 'Favorites' list.
screenshots:
Screen 1: Google Maps app showing the current location with a blue dot on the map. The search bar is at the top with 'Search here' placeholder text. Bottom bar shows: Explore, Go, Saved, Contribute, Updates tabs. The map displays surrounding streets, buildings in gray blocks, and a few labeled businesses.

Screen 2: Search results for 'pharmacy'. The map shows 5 red pin markers at pharmacy locations. A bottom sheet lists results: 'CVS Pharmacy (0.3 mi, Open)', 'Walgreens (0.5 mi, Open)', 'Rite Aid (0.8 mi, Closing soon)', 'Local Pharmacy Plus (1.1 mi, Open)'. Each result shows name, distance, hours, and star rating.

Screen 3: Place detail card for 'CVS Pharmacy' (nearest result). Shows: store photo, name, 4.1 stars (892 reviews), 'Open until 10 PM', address '123 Main St'. Action buttons: Directions (blue), Start, Save (bookmark icon), Share, Call. The 'Directions' button is highlighted.

Screen 4: Save to list dialog is open. Lists shown: 'Favorites' (with star icon, 8 places), 'Want to go' (3 places), 'Starred places' (12 places), '+ New list'. The agent has tapped 'Favorites'. A brief toast message at the bottom says 'Saved to Favorites'.
trajectory:
Step 1: [TAP] Search bar at top of Maps screen
Step 2: [TYPE] 'pharmacy'
Step 3: [TAP] Search/Enter on keyboard
Step 4: [TAP] 'CVS Pharmacy' (nearest result, 0.3 mi)
Step 5: [TAP] 'Directions' button on place card
Step 6: [TAP] Back arrow to return to place detail
Step 7: [TAP] 'Save' (bookmark icon) button
Step 8: [TAP] 'Favorites' from save-to-list dialog
Step 9: STOP — Task complete
final_state: CVS Pharmacy (0.3 mi) shown on map with directions route displayed. Location saved to 'Favorites' list — bookmark icon is now filled/highlighted on the place card. Toast confirmation: 'Saved to Favorites'. Directions available showing walking route of approximately 6 minutes.

text: Open the Chrome browser, navigate to a news website, switch to the 'Reading Mode' or simplified view, and bookmark the current article.
screenshots:
Screen 1: Chrome browser open showing the new tab page. The Google search bar is centered in the upper portion. Below are shortcut tiles for frequently visited sites: Google News, YouTube, Reddit, Wikipedia, Gmail, Drive. The tab counter shows '3' in the top-right, and the three-dot menu is at the far right.

Screen 2: The agent has navigated to a news website. The article page shows a headline 'Global Climate Summit Reaches Historic Agreement on Emissions Targets'. Below: author name, publication date, a large header image of world leaders at a conference table. Article body text is partially visible but cluttered with sidebar ads, related articles widgets, and newsletter signup banners.

Screen 3: Chrome's three-dot overflow menu is open. Options visible: New tab, New incognito tab, Bookmarks, Recent tabs, History, Downloads, Share, Find in page, Translate, Desktop site, Settings. Near the bottom: 'Simplified view' option with a reader icon. The agent taps 'Simplified view'.

Screen 4: Article displayed in simplified/reader mode. Clean white background with the headline, author, and article body text in a comfortable reading font. No ads, sidebars, or clutter. A filled star (bookmark) icon is visible in the address bar area, indicating the page has been bookmarked. The URL bar shows the article URL.
trajectory:
Step 1: [TAP] Google News shortcut tile on new tab page
Step 2: [SCROLL] Through news homepage to find an article
Step 3: [TAP] Article headline 'Global Climate Summit Reaches Historic Agreement'
Step 4: [TAP] Three-dot menu in top-right corner
Step 5: [TAP] 'Simplified view' option
Step 6: [TAP] Three-dot menu again
Step 7: [TAP] Star/Bookmark icon to bookmark the article
Step 8: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Chrome displaying article in simplified/reader mode — clean layout without ads or distractions. Article 'Global Climate Summit Reaches Historic Agreement on Emissions Targets' is fully readable. Page bookmarked — star icon is filled. Bookmark saved to 'Mobile bookmarks' folder.

text: Set an alarm for 6:30 AM with the label 'Morning Run' and select the 'Bright Morning' alarm tone in the Clock app.
screenshots:
Screen 1: Clock app open on the Alarm tab. The bottom navigation shows four tabs: Alarm (selected, bell icon), Clock (world icon), Timer (hourglass icon), Stopwatch (stopwatch icon). Two existing alarms are shown: '7:00 AM — Work' (toggle ON) and '9:00 AM — Weekend' (toggle OFF). The floating action button ('+') is in the bottom-right.

Screen 2: Time picker dialog for setting the new alarm. A circular clock face is displayed with hour numbers. The agent has set the time to 6:30 — the hour hand points to 6 and the minute indicator shows 30. 'AM' is selected (highlighted) on the left side. 'Cancel' and 'OK' buttons are at the bottom of the dialog.

Screen 3: Alarm detail/edit screen for the new 6:30 AM alarm. Options shown: toggle switch (ON), time display '6:30 AM', 'Repeat' (set to 'Never'), 'Alarm sound' (showing default tone), 'Vibrate' toggle, 'Label' field. The agent has tapped the Label field and typed 'Morning Run'. The keyboard is visible.

Screen 4: Alarm sound picker screen. A list of alarm tones is displayed with radio buttons: 'Default (Cesium)', 'Bright Morning' (selected — radio filled blue), 'Early Bird', 'Gentle Breeze', 'Sunrise Melody', 'Digital Pulse'. A preview/play button is next to each tone. The back arrow is in the top-left.
trajectory:
Step 1: [TAP] Floating action button ('+') to create new alarm
Step 2: [TAP] Hour '6' on clock face
Step 3: [TAP] Minute '30' on clock face
Step 4: [TAP] 'AM' selector
Step 5: [TAP] 'OK' to confirm time
Step 6: [TAP] 'Label' field
Step 7: [TYPE] 'Morning Run'
Step 8: [TAP] 'Alarm sound' option
Step 9: [SCROLL] Down to find 'Bright Morning' tone
Step 10: [TAP] 'Bright Morning' radio button
Step 11: [TAP] Back arrow to return to alarm detail
Step 12: STOP — Task complete
final_state: New alarm set for 6:30 AM with label 'Morning Run'. Alarm tone set to 'Bright Morning'. Alarm toggle is ON. The alarm list now shows three alarms: '6:30 AM — Morning Run' (ON), '7:00 AM — Work' (ON), '9:00 AM — Weekend' (OFF).

text: In the Files app, create a new folder called 'Work Documents', move 3 PDF files from Downloads into it, and sort the folder contents by date modified.
screenshots:
Screen 1: Files app main screen. Categories shown at top: Images, Videos, Audio, Documents, Downloads, Apps. Below: Recent files section showing 8 recently accessed files with thumbnails, names, and sizes. Internal storage usage bar shows 78% used (62GB of 128GB). The hamburger menu icon is in the top-left.

Screen 2: Downloads folder open, showing 12 files. File list includes: 'quarterly_report.pdf' (2.4MB), 'meeting_notes.pdf' (156KB), 'project_proposal.pdf' (1.8MB), 'vacation_photo.jpg' (4.1MB), 'presentation.pptx' (8.2MB), 'invoice_march.pdf' (89KB), and others. Three-dot menu is in the top-right. The agent long-presses on 'quarterly_report.pdf'.

Screen 3: Multi-selection mode is active. Three PDF files are selected (checkmarks visible): 'quarterly_report.pdf', 'meeting_notes.pdf', 'project_proposal.pdf'. A selection toolbar at top shows '3 selected' with action icons: Share, Delete, More (three-dot). The agent taps the three-dot menu, revealing options: Copy to, Move to, Rename, Details.

Screen 4: Files app showing the 'Work Documents' folder contents. Three PDF files are listed: 'project_proposal.pdf' (Mar 28), 'quarterly_report.pdf' (Mar 25), 'meeting_notes.pdf' (Mar 20). A sort indicator in the toolbar shows 'Date modified' with a downward arrow (newest first). The folder path breadcrumb shows 'Internal storage > Work Documents'.
trajectory:
Step 1: [TAP] 'Downloads' category on Files main screen
Step 2: [TAP] Three-dot menu in top-right
Step 3: [TAP] 'New folder' option
Step 4: [TYPE] 'Work Documents'
Step 5: [TAP] 'Create' button
Step 6: [TAP] Back to return to Downloads
Step 7: [LONG PRESS] 'quarterly_report.pdf' to enter selection mode
Step 8: [TAP] 'meeting_notes.pdf' to add to selection
Step 9: [TAP] 'project_proposal.pdf' to add to selection
Step 10: [TAP] Three-dot menu in selection toolbar
Step 11: [TAP] 'Move to' option
Step 12: [TAP] Navigate to 'Work Documents' folder
Step 13: [TAP] 'Move' button to confirm
Step 14: [TAP] Sort icon in toolbar
Step 15: [TAP] 'Date modified' sort option
Step 16: STOP — Task complete
final_state: Files app showing 'Work Documents' folder with 3 PDF files sorted by date modified (newest first): project_proposal.pdf (Mar 28), quarterly_report.pdf (Mar 25), meeting_notes.pdf (Mar 20). Files successfully moved from Downloads. The Downloads folder no longer contains these 3 PDFs.
